title: ‘We’re not concerned about Boston’: Pistons’ head coach referenced Celtics amid four-game losing streak
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Pistons head coach J.B. Bickerstaff, despite a four-game losing streak, stated they're not concerned about the Celtics.
Detroit Pistons' Eastern Conference lead is down to 2.5 games as the Boston Celtics gain momentum with Jayson Tatum's return.
The Pistons (45-18) face the Brooklyn Nets (16-47) on Tuesday, while the Celtics (43-21) play the Spurs in San Antonio.

title: Boston’s Patricia Cornwell sees her books land on screen with ‘Scarpetta’
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Patricia Cornwell's 'Scarpetta' series, featuring forensic investigator Kay Scarpetta, premieres March 11 on Prime Video.
Nicole Kidman stars as Scarpetta, while Jamie Lee Curtis plays Scarpetta's eccentric sister Dorothy in the TV adaptation.
Cornwell's upcoming memoir, 'True Crime,' discusses her life experiences and journey to becoming a successful novelist.

title: Patriots free agency live updates: Tracking New England’s top offseason pickups
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots have over $50 million in cap space to improve their roster for the upcoming season.
New England released LB Jahlani Tavai and traded starting center Garrett Bradbury ahead of free agency.
The team is rumored to be pursuing Seahawks linebacker Boye Mafe with a potential $48 million contract.

title: Boston Marathon runner aims to complete marathons in all 50 states
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Zara Harding, 46, from Nevada, aims to complete marathons in all 50 states; she only has 10 states left.
Harding has run over a dozen marathons since recovering from colorectal cancer diagnosed in early 2021.
She is running the Boston Marathon to raise $12,000 for the Dana-Farber Marathon Challenge Team supporting cancer research.

title: Judge dismisses lawsuit of Babson College student mistakenly deported to Honduras
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Judge Richard Stearns dismissed Babson student Any Lucia Lopez Belloza's lawsuit after she declined a government flight back to the U.S.
Lopez Belloza was mistakenly deported to Honduras despite a court order, due to miscommunication by immigration officials.
Her legal team has filed an appeal, arguing she should have been allowed a student visa to continue her studies in Massachusetts.

title: NBA coach dubs Derrick White as a ‘top-5 player’ in NBA
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson praised Derrick White as a top-five player in the NBA using advanced analytics.
Derrick White is averaging 17.4 points, 4.4 rebounds, and 5.7 assists this season for the Boston Celtics.
With Jayson Tatum back from injury, Derrick White resumes his versatile role, impacting games defensively.

title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum dynamic shines in Celtics win over Cavaliers: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum scored 20 points, leading the Boston Celtics to a 109-98 win over the Cleveland Cavaliers.
Jaylen Brown excelled with 23 points, nine rebounds, and eight assists, pivotal in the Celtics' victory.
Boston's strong defense contributed to the Cavaliers' poor shooting, with Cleveland missing 18 of 19 three-point attempts.

title: North Kingstown, R.I. police officer dies by suicide, department says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Kingstown police officer Brittany Dougal, 29, died by suicide after a battle with mental illness on March 4.
Dougal was a recent graduate of the Rhode Island Municipal Police Training Academy and began work in North Kingstown.
Her family requests donations to North Kingstown Animal Shelter or Tunnel to Towers Foundation instead of flowers.

title: 9 under-the-radar players for Patriots to consider in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots considering under-the-radar players like Vikings WR Jalen Nailor and Giants TE Daniel Bellinger in free agency.
Daniel Bellinger, a potential TE target for the Patriots, had 19 receptions and two touchdowns last season with the Giants.
EDGE options like Joseph Ossai (Bengals) and Al-Quadin Muhammad (Lions) are potential targets for Patriots' defensive depth.

title: Did Tom Brady prevent Patriots from acquiring Maxx Crosby? One report suggests possibility.
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Tom Brady, a minority owner of the Raiders, reportedly opposed trading Maxx Crosby to the Patriots.
The Raiders traded Crosby to the Ravens for two first-round picks, including the 14th pick in the 2026 draft.
The Patriots may seek edge rushers in free agency, linking to Jaelan Phillips, Boye Mafe, and K'Lavon Chaisson.
